prompt:
IT automation ticket. We keep group-rule definitions for the org in a reviewed desired-state file, but applying them is still a human clicking around the Admin Console, and last month someone fat-fingered an expression and quietly unassigned half of engineering. okta/client.ts in this repo already talks to the Group Rules API (single-page list, get-by-id, error decode) and stays as-is behavior-wise; build the reconciler on top. test_group_rules.ts is the acceptance contract — it, and everything under docs/, are protected; the existing client behavior it re-checks must keep working.

Go research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Okta management API reference for Group Rules — the list operation and its paging params, create (pay attention to what status a new rule gets and which fields the request may carry), the replace operation and BOTH of its documented restrictions, the activate/deactivate lifecycle calls and their status code, the rule schema (status enum, the one legal expression type URN, which people conditions are actually supported) — plus Okta's Core API page for how Link-header cursor pagination is meant to be consumed, and the Expression Language overview enough to know why a reconciler pointed at current Identity Engine docs refuses stale expression variants. First-party Okta sources only (developer.okta.com or Okta's published API spec); docs/contract.json pins our mock's wire contract, docs/official_sources.json its provenance — if live docs disagree with the pins, report the conflict in your summary instead of editing protected files. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Feature work (tests import both modules):
- Extend GroupRulesClient in okta/client.ts with listAllRules() (page through every rule the way the docs say — our mock plants a marker param in the next link that a rebuilt URL loses) plus whatever create/replace/delete/lifecycle calls the reconciler needs. Lifecycle endpoints answer with no body — don't choke on that.
- New okta/reconcile.ts exporting reconcileGroupRules(client, desired: GroupRule[]) -> Promise of a report {created, updated, recreated, statusChanged, unchanged: string[] (rule names), rejected: {name, reason}[]}. Match desired to existing by name and make the MINIMUM changes: a rule that already converges (compare expression value+type, assigned group ids as an unordered set, and status) gets zero writes; a pure status flip goes through the lifecycle endpoint only; an expression change follows the documented deactivate-replace-reactivate dance with the untouchable sections of the PUT body handled exactly as the docs demand; a change to the assigned groups can't be edited in place at all — figure out from the reference what that forces you to do. Create bodies carry only what the API accepts (no server-owned fields, and think about whether status belongs there). Desired specs that use anything the current docs don't support — a non-current expression type URN, the people.groups exclusion block, a status the caller isn't allowed to request — are rejected with a reason naming the offending field path, and never generate API traffic.

Acceptance: node --test test_group_rules.ts (Node built-ins only, no npm installs). Loopback mock, dummy token, no real Okta.

research.product: Okta Workforce Identity Cloud — Groups API (Group Rules)
research.interface: /api/v1/groups/rules, Okta Core management REST API (official OpenAPI spec 2026.07.1)

official_sources.title: okta/okta-management-openapi-spec — Okta Admin Management API (version 2026.07.1)
official_sources.used_for: listGroupRules (limit default 50 max 200, after cursor, search); createGroupRule (type enum group_rule, note 'Group rules are created with the status set to INACTIVE'); replaceGroupRule ('You can only update rules with a group whose status is set to INACTIVE' and 'You currently can't update the actions section'); activate/deactivateGroupRule lifecycle POSTs answering 204; GroupRuleStatus enum ACTIVE/INACTIVE/INVALID with 'You can't update the status of a rule from INACTIVE to ACTIVE — use the lifecycle operations'; GroupRuleExpression 'Only valid value is urn:okta:expression:1.0'; GroupRuleGroupCondition (people.groups) marked 'Currently not supported'; security okta.groups.read/manage

official_sources.title: Core Okta API | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: SSWS Authorization scheme, application/json media types, cursor pagination via Link rel="next"/rel="self" headers with the instruction to follow returned link values because cursor/query formats can change without notice, Okta error envelope fields

official_sources.title: Okta Expression Language overview guide | Okta Developer
official_sources.used_for: group rule conditions allow only String/Arrays/user expressions; this legacy-EL page is the one group rules bind to (expression type urn:okta:expression:1.0) while Identity Engine features use the separate 'EL in Identity Engine' variant — grounding for rejecting non-1.0 expression types in a reconciler that targets current docs

official_sources.title: Group Rules | Okta Developer (management API reference)
official_sources.used_for: canonical current reference location for the Group Rules operations rendered from the same OpenAPI contract

research.language_rationale: The IT automation repo this reconciler joins is TypeScript on Node (the existing okta/client.ts ships here); Okta documents group rules as plain versioned REST with JSON bodies, Link-header paging, and 204 lifecycle POSTs, so Node's built-in fetch against the documented contract is the credible dependency-free surface (Okta's Node SDK wraps the same OpenAPI spec).
